DrugDoseResultVolumeConcentrationDilutionActions

DrugDoseResultVolumeConcentrationDilutionActions

SEIZURES
DrugDoseResultVolume / NotesMax / DilutionActions
MidazolamIV/IO0.15 mg/kg—— mL of 1mg/mL2mL of 15mg/3mL → 10mL
MidazolamBuccal0.3 mg/kg—Buccal preparation
Levetiracetam (Keppra)40 mg/kg—Equal vol 0.9% NaCl — IV over 10 minMax 3g
Phenytoin20 mg/kg loading—— mLMax 1.5g
Phenobarbitone20 mg/kg loading—Dilute to 20mg/mL — over 20 minMax 1g
RAISED ICP
DrugDoseResultVolumeNotesActions
Mannitol0.25–1 g/kg—— mLmL of 20% Mannitol

DrugDoseResultVolumeConcentrationNotesActions
Adrenaline (Epinephrine)0.5–1 mcg/kg——mL of 10mcg/mL1mg in 100mL NaCl 0.9%

Max 500mcg
OTHER DRUGS
DrugDoseResultVolumeConcentrationNotesActions
Adenosine (1st)100 mcg/kg—— mLmL of 3mg/mLAdult 6mg
Adenosine (2nd)200 mcg/kg—— mLmL of 3mg/mLAdult 12mg
Adenosine (3rd)300 mcg/kg—— mLmL of 3mg/mLMax 18mg
Calcium Gluconate 10%0.5 mL/kg—— mLmL of 10% CaGlucMax 20mL
Sodium Bicarbonate 8.4%1 mL/kg (1 mmol/kg)—— mLmL of 8.4% NaHCO₃
Magnesium Sulphate 50%0.1 mL/kg—— mLmL of 50% MgSO₄Max 4mL
⚠ CLINICAL DISCLAIMER: Drug doses are a guide only. Clinicians must verify doses for age, weight and clinical condition. Based on Perth Children's Hospital ED & APLS (Aust & NZ) guidelines (Aug 2023). Always double-check calculations between patients.

POST INTUBATION INFUSIONS — Standard Concentration
Drug / PreparationCalculated DoseRangeActions
Morphine Standard Concentration*
—		—
Bolus: 20 mcg/kg (max 1mg) q10min		10–40 mcg/kg/hr
Fentanyl Standard Concentration*
—		—
Bolus: 1–2 mcg/kg (max 100mcg) q5min		2–10 mcg/kg/hr
Midazolam Standard Concentration
—		—
—		50–200 mcg/kg/hr
Propofol
Neat (10mg/mL)		—1–4 mg/kg/hr
Ketamine Standard Concentration
—		—
Bolus: 1–2 mg/kg prn		1–6 mcg/kg/MIN
*Morphine and fentanyl infusion concentrations and rates are for post intubation sedation NOT analgesia.

—
Noradrenaline (Norepinephrine)6mg in 1000mL NaCl 0.9% or Glucose 5%1mL/kg/hr = 0.1mcg/kg/min
Metaraminol0.15mg/kg (max 10mg) → 50mL NaCl 0.9%
—		0.05–0.5 mcg/kg/min · 1–10mL/hr

Deficit corrected over 24h
FLUID PROTOCOLS & BOLUS REFERENCE
Fluid / IndicationDoseNotesActions
Resuscitation Bolus20 mL/kg NaCl 0.9%Give over 5–10 min. Repeat up to 60 mL/kg total.
DKA Resuscitation10 mL/kg NaCl 0.9%Over 30 min. Avoid aggressive hydration.
Neonatal Bolus10 mL/kg NaCl 0.9%Over 30–60 min. Monitor carefully.
Maintenance SolutionNaCl 0.9% + 5% DextroseStandard maintenance fluid for most paediatric patients.
Mild (<3%)
No physical signs
Thirst
Dry mucous membranes
Reduced urine output
Moderate (4–6%)
Dry mucous membranes
Reduced urine output
Tachycardia
Sunken eyes
Diminished skin turgor
Altered neuro status
Severe (7–10%)
Above signs PLUS:
↓ Peripheral perfusion
Anuria
Hypotension
Circulatory collapse
NOTE: Clinician must double check age and weight before prescribing fluid. Clinical condition may require different regimen.

HIGH ALERT DRUG CAUTION: These medications bear a heightened risk of causing significant patient harm when used in error. Always perform independent double-check before administration. Verify: drug name · dose · concentration · rate · route · patient identity. Sources: ISMP High Alert Medications · UpToDate · Medscape · BNF for Children · Taketomo 30th ed. 2023 · WHO Essential Medicines
